task_completion.description: Verify the 'Sample Order Lines' dataset is loaded into Oracle Analytics Desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Sample Order Lines' dataset should be visible in the data elements pane on the left side of the Oracle Analytics Desktop interface.

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of the 'Corporate Revenue' and 'Consumer Revenue' calculated measures.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'Corporate Revenue' and 'Consumer Revenue' listed under 'My Calculations' in the data pane, or actively used in the visualization grammar pane.

task_completion.description: Verify the visualization is a Grouped Bar Chart.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main canvas should display a bar chart with bars grouped side-by-side (not stacked), and the visualization type icon in the grammar pane should indicate a Bar Chart.

task_completion.description: Verify the chart compares the calculated measures by 'Product Category'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the grammar/properties pane, 'Product Category' should be placed in the Category (X-axis) section, and both 'Corporate Revenue' and 'Consumer Revenue' should be in the Values (Y-axis) section.

task_completion.description: Verify the chart title is exactly 'Corporate vs Consumer Revenue by Product Category'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The title at the top of the visualization canvas should read exactly 'Corporate vs Consumer Revenue by Product Category'.

task_completion.description: Verify the workbook is saved as 'segment_comparison.dva' in the correct directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog showing the file name 'segment_comparison' and the path 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents', OR the top application title bar showing 'segment_comparison' as the active workbook name.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Oracle Analytics Desktop GUI to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Oracle Analytics Desktop interface with menus, drag-and-drop actions, or dialog boxes, rather than terminal commands or scripts.

integrity.description: Verify the chart is genuinely rendered by the software.
integrity.visual_evidence: The chart should appear within the Oracle Analytics canvas with standard UI elements (axes, legends, tooltips) rather than looking like an imported static image.

integrity.description: Verify the file was saved through the application's native save functionality.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the Oracle Analytics 'Save' or 'Save As' dialog box, or the updated workbook name in the application header, rather than a command-line file creation.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected filename is 'segment_comparison.dva' saved in 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\'. The calculated measures must be named exactly 'Corporate Revenue' and 'Consumer Revenue'. The chart title must be exactly 'Corporate vs Consumer Revenue by Product Category'. Note: The prompt asks to use a 'Revenue' column in the CASE WHEN logic, but the dataset only contains a 'Sales' column; the agent should have adapted to use 'Sales' or successfully created the measures regardless. Focus on the presence of the correctly named measures and the final chart configuration.
